icd10_codes.description: Other congenital deformities of skull, face and jaw

indications.name: Multiple Congenital Anomalies
indications.custom_value: Left congenital diaphragmatic hernia (Bochdalek type, surgically repaired DOL 2 by Dr. Karen Sato, pediatric surgery), tetralogy of Fallot (confirmed on echocardiogram DOL 1 — VSD, overriding aorta, right ventricular outflow tract obstruction, right ventricular hypertrophy), Pierre Robin sequence with severe micrognathia (mandibular length <3rd percentile) requiring prone positioning for airway management, respiratory failure requiring mechanical ventilation since birth.

clinical_info.prior_testing_details: 1) STAT chromosomal microarray ordered DOL 1 — results PENDING (expected within 5 days). 2) FISH for 22q11.2 deletion (DiGeorge syndrome) — NEGATIVE (reported DOL 3). FISH was prioritized given phenotypic overlap with 22q11.2 deletion syndrome (cardiac defect + micrognathia), but negative result does not rule out other microdeletions or single-gene disorders.
clinical_info.supplemental_notes:
4-day-old female in NICU at Lurie Children's Hospital on mechanical ventilation (conventional ventilation, FiO2 0.45, PEEP 6). Born at 37+2 weeks via emergency C-section for fetal distress (persistent late decelerations). Birth weight 2,650g (25th percentile). Apgar 4/6/7.

Genetics consulted DOL 1 by NICU attending Dr. Marcus Webb. Evaluated by Dr. Rachel Okonkwo, MD, FACMG (board-certified Clinical Geneticist, ABMGG #2013-2104) on DOL 1. Geneticist assessment: 'Multiple congenital anomalies involving diaphragm, heart, and craniofacial structures. Phenotype is not classic for 22q11.2 deletion (no thymic hypoplasia, calcium normal). Differential includes: Fryns syndrome, Cornelia de Lange syndrome, CHARGE syndrome, Kabuki syndrome, and novel genetic etiologies. Rapid WGS recommended to guide surgical planning — cardiac surgical team needs to know if there is a syndromic diagnosis that affects surgical risk or prognosis before proceeding with TOF repair.'

Pre-test genetic counseling completed DOL 1 with both parents by Amanda Liu, MS, CGC (ABGC #2020-CGC-05182). Parents counseled on rapid timeline, possible outcomes, limitations, implications for future pregnancies. Both parents consented. Informed consent on file.

Rapid WGS ordered as proband-only (no parental samples at this time) to maximize turnaround. Parental samples collected and banked for follow-up trio analysis if needed.
clinical_info.family_history: Non-consanguineous parents. Mother (age 29, African American) — healthy, G2P1 (prior uncomplicated pregnancy, healthy 3-year-old son). Father (age 32, European descent) — healthy. No known family history of congenital anomalies, genetic conditions, or congenital heart disease on either side. No history of pregnancy loss.
clinical_info.additional_info:
URGENT — acutely ill neonate. Multidisciplinary team conference scheduled DOL 10 with cardiology, genetics, neonatology, and surgery to determine surgical timing for TOF repair. Genetic diagnosis will directly impact: 1) Surgical risk stratification — some syndromic diagnoses carry higher perioperative mortality, 2) Prognosis for neurodevelopment — affects goals-of-care discussions with family, 3) Surveillance protocol — additional organ systems to monitor depending on diagnosis, 4) Recurrence risk counseling for future pregnancies.

Note: Aetna considers rapid WES/WGS for acutely ill patients age 21 or under — this patient qualifies as acutely ill neonate in NICU. However, Aetna's policy states rapid testing is covered when standard criteria are also met.

care_team.institution_name: Ann & Robert H. Lurie Children's Hospital
